svg.text: EAGLE Speculative Decoding Full Data Flow
Draft-Verify cycle: Target Prefill → Draft K steps → Tree build → Target Verify → Token accept → Draft Extend

g.text: STEP 1 · Target Prefill (Initialization)
Target model Prefills the full Prompt
• Target model runs Prefill Forward and emits the first Token
• Initialize EagleDraftInput: verified_id = first_token, hidden_states = target_hidden[-1]
• Draft and Target share req_to_token_pool and token_to_kv_pool
Draft model shares Target's Embedding and LM Head; Hidden States serve as Draft input (note: EAGLE3 does not share lm_head, see eagle_worker.py:70-71)
STEP 2 · Draft Phase (K-step generation)
EAGLEWorker.draft() — loop speculative_num_steps times
• Each step:
draft_input.prepare_for_decode() # pre-allocate KV Cache
logits = draft_worker.forward(draft_input) # Draft model Forward
topk_p, topk_index = topk_probs_from_logits(logits, topk=self.topk) # Top-K sampling
draft_input.topk_p = topk_p; draft_input.topk_index = topk_index
• Accumulate Top-K candidates from all steps into all_draft_tokens and all_draft_probs
• Draft model uses DRAFT_EXTEND ForwardMode; each step writes its own KV Cache
Draft model is lightweight (typically 1-2B params); K-step Forward total is far less than one Target Forward
STEP 3 · Tree Construction (LCRS structure)
build_tree_kernel_efficient() — two-phase Tree build
• Phase 1: build LCRS pointers via Pallas Kernel build_eagle_tree_structure()
tree_mask, position_ids, retrieve_indices = build_eagle_tree_structure(...)
- tree_mask: Tree Attention Mask, lets each node see its ancestor path
- position_ids: position of each node in the sequence (Root=0, children increment)
- retrieve_indices: mapping from Draft KV Cache indices to Tree nodes
• Phase 2: extract corresponding KV from Draft KV Cache and rearrange into Tree layout
LCRS (Left-Child Right-Sibling): each node stores pointers to first child and next sibling
STEP 4 · Target Verify (one Forward verifies the entire Tree)
EAGLEWorker.verify() — Target model verification
• Build EagleVerifyInput: contains Tree Mask, Draft Tokens, Draft Probs
target_logits = target_worker.forward(verify_input) # TARGET_VERIFY ForwardMode
• Target model verifies all branches in one Forward via Tree Attention Mask
• Each Tree node receives Logits output from the Target model
• Returns Target Hidden States (consumed by next Draft round)
Tree Attention Mask ensures each node sees only the path from Root to itself, preserving causality
STEP 5 · Token Acceptance (Greedy / Stochastic)
Greedy Verify (default)
• Pallas Kernel: verify_tree_greedy()
target_token = argmax(target_logits)
if target_token == draft_token:
accept, continue verifying next node along Tree path
else:
reject, truncate Tree, use target_token as Bonus Token
• Verify node-by-node along Tree path starting from Root
• Returns accept_length and the accepted Token sequence
Greedy mode: strict match, no distribution drift
Stochastic Verify (optional)
• Pallas Kernel: tree_speculative_sampling_target_only()
if p_target(draft_token) > threshold_single:
accept (fast path)
elif cumulative_p > threshold_acc:
accept (cumulative probability check)
else:
reject, residual = relu(q_draft - p_target)
resample Bonus Token from residual distribution
When threshold_acc=1.0, reduces to strict Rejection Sampling (unbiased)
STEP 6 · KV Cache Management
Trim un-accepted KV Cache entries
• Draft and Target share the same token_to_kv_pool
• Trim indices in req_to_token_pool based on accept_length
new_seq_len = old_seq_len + accept_length
• Un-accepted Draft KV Cache is freed (reclaimed by Allocator)
Shared Pool avoids KV data copies between Draft and Target, saving memory and bandwidth
STEP 7 · Draft Extend (update Draft state)
EAGLEWorker.draft_extend_after_verify()
• Update EagleDraftInput:
draft_input.verified_id = last_accepted_token
draft_input.hidden_states = target_hidden_states[-1]
draft_input.accept_length = accept_length
• Draft model runs one DRAFT_EXTEND Forward over all accepted Tokens
STEP 8 · Loop or Finish
Check if sequence is complete
• If EOS Token reached or max_new_tokens hit → end generation
• Otherwise return to STEP 2, start next Draft-Verify round
Each round accepts 1~K Tokens (avg 2-3), boosting throughput 2-3x
Key Design Notes
Why a Tree instead of a linear sequence?
• Draft model emits Top-K candidates per step (instead of just Top-1), forming a branching Tree
• Tree Attention Mask lets the Target model verify all branches in one Forward, raising the chance at least one path is accepted
• E.g., K=4 steps with Top-5 each → up to 5^4=625 leaves (pruned to ~20 nodes in practice)
Benefits of a shared KV Cache Pool
• Draft and Target use the same token_to_kv_pool, avoiding KV data copies between models
• After Verify, indices in req_to_token_pool are trimmed and un-accepted KV is reclaimed by the Allocator
• Saves memory bandwidth and reduces Host-Device sync overhead
EAGLE Algorithm Highlights
• Draft model shares Target's Embedding and LM Head, reducing parameter count
• Draft uses Target's Hidden States as extra input, improving candidate quality
• Supports both Greedy (strict match) and Stochastic (probabilistic accept) verification
